title: The Last Glen
content:
혻혻혻혻Hist! once more!
혻혻혻혻Listen, Pausanias!占폗ye, 占퐐is Callicles!
혻혻혻혻I know those notes among a thousand.혻혻혻혻Hark!
혻혻혻혻
혻혻혻혻CALLICLES
혻혻혻혻
혻혻혻혻(Sings unseen, from below.)
혻혻혻혻
혻혻혻혻The track winds down to the clear stream,
혻혻혻혻To cross the sparkling shallows; there
혻혻혻혻The, cattle love to gather, on their way
혻혻혻혻To the high mountain pastures, and to stay,
혻혻혻혻Till the rough cow-herds drive them past,
혻혻혻혻Knee-deep in the cool ford; for 占퐐is the last
혻혻혻혻Of all the woody, high, well-water占폻 dells
혻혻혻혻On Etna; and the beam
혻혻혻혻Of noon is broken there by chestnut boughs
혻혻혻혻Down its steep verdant sides; the air
혻혻혻혻Is freshen占폻 by the leaping stream, which throws
혻혻혻혻Eternal showers of spray on the moss占폻 roots
혻혻혻혻Of trees, and veins of turf, and long dark shoots
혻혻혻혻Of ivy-plants, and fragrant hanging bells
혻혻혻혻Of hyacinths, and on late anemonies,
혻혻혻혻That muffle its wet banks; but glade,
혻혻혻혻And stream, and sward, and chestnut trees,
혻혻혻혻End here; Etna beyond, in the broad glare
혻혻혻혻Of the hot noon, without a shade,
혻혻혻혻Slope behind slope, up to the peak, lies bare;
혻혻혻혻The peak, round which the white clouds play.
혻혻혻혻In such a glen, on such a day,
혻혻혻혻On Pelion, on the grassy ground,
혻혻혻혻Chiron, the aged Centaur, lay,
혻혻혻혻The young Achilles standing by.
혻혻혻혻The Centaur taught him to explore
혻혻혻혻The mountains; where the glens are dry,
혻혻혻혻And the tired Centaurs come to rest,
혻혻혻혻And where the soaking springs abound,
혻혻혻혻And the straight ashes grow for spears,
혻혻혻혻And where the hill-goats come to feed,
혻혻혻혻And the sea-eagles build their nest.
혻혻혻혻He show占폻 him Phthia far away,
혻혻혻혻And said: O boy, I taught this lore
혻혻혻혻To Peleus, in long distant years!
혻혻혻혻He told him of the Gods, the stars,
혻혻혻혻The tides;占폸nd then of mortal wars,
혻혻혻혻And of the life which heroes lead
혻혻혻혻Before they reach the Elysian place
혻혻혻혻And rest in the immortal mead;
혻혻혻혻And all the wisdom of his race.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Matthew Arnold represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Matthew Arnold's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
